title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in accessible human and machine report system and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.
Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Structured Alternative
steps.instruction:
Order the Structured Alternative Bookbot and Python corpus-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Structured Alternative.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Structured Alternative assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Structured Alternative, keep Structured Alternative connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 2990 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.

content.text: Order work so Structured Alternative has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

For structured alternative · fault isolation, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof.
A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Structured Alternative.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Structured Alternative
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-1indr9" that uses case 4356 to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Structured Alternative.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Structured Alternative into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Structured Alternative, keep Structured Alternative connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-1indr9
# Competency: bookbot-structured-alternative.
# Case 4356: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_structured_alternative_1indr9(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1indr9 = "1indr9-9rdni1-4356"
    assert evidence_variant_1indr9
    evidence_axes_1indr9 = (
        "corpus1indr9",
        "policy9rdni1",
        "revision4356",
        "command1indr94356",
        "report9rdni14356",
        "test43561indr9",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1indr9)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.
